action.reasoning: Observation: val_search isolated the remaining bug: legacy checkpoints with horizon_mixer fail because Conv1d is receiving [B, N, H] instead of [B, H, N]; baseline/end-conv load but produce terrible MAE, so exact legacy forward semantics are still wrong. Analysis: the strongest prior public score came from ema_weighted.pt/improved.pt, both legacy-family checkpoints. With only 2 iterations left, the highest-value action is a surgical fix to legacy forward: squeeze the head output to [B,H,N], apply horizon_mixer as Conv1d over horizon channels with input [B,H,N], then optional horizon scale/bias and return [B,H,N]. Also make variant inference prefer legacy when head.* exists. Then immediately run val_search and local predict smoke test; if validation returns near prior ~3 range, submit ema_weighted.pt right away. Decision: patch model.py minimally but exactly for legacy semantics, verify on val and local predict, and submit ema_weighted.pt if fixed.
action.command: python val_search.py && python predict.py --data_path local_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ema_weighted.pt --output local_test/output.json
